An extreme

rule_text: To hedge a long call position neutrally, sell call options at a higher strike in a ratio equal to the inverse of the hedge ratio differential between the two strikes.

Calculate hedge ratio for short strike (example: 0.25 for January 35)
Calculate neutral ratio as: lower hedge ratio divided by higher hedge ratio (example: 0.25 / 0.50 = 0.5, or expressed as 2:1 short to long)
Sell call options at higher strike in the calculated ratio against long call position

Trader expects the differential to widen (example: from 7 cents to 12 cents by early winter)
Market environment shows potential for high volatility in the underlying products
Combined time value premium of call and put is less than potential profit from expected widening

parameters.expected_widening: 5.00 points minimum to recover time value premium paid
